name: Pickled Onion Rings
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, cut into thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate the onion slices into rings.
2. Place the rings in a two-cup sterilized jar.
3. Pour boiling water over the onions to cover them.
4. Allow the mixture to cool, then drain.
5. Mix together vinegar, water, sugar, salt, and hot pepper sauce in a separate bowl.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil.
7. Pour the hot liquid over the onion rings.
8. Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 2 days before using.
9. Store in the refrigerator up to 2 months.
Good with hamburger, cheese sandwich, egg salad sandwich, and as a pickle with regular meals. Makes about 2 cups, serving 2-3 onion rings each (extra approximately 3 grams carbohydrate, 12 calories).
